equations.label: eq:capm
equations.description: CAPM: expected excess return of asset $i$ equals market beta times the equity risk premium.

qa.question: What does beta_i measure in the CAPM?
qa.answer: The covariance of asset return with market return, divided by market return variance

equations.label: eq:ff3
equations.latex: R_{it} - R_{ft} = \alpha_i + b_i (R_{mt} - R_{ft}) + s_i \text{SMB}_t + h_i \text{HML}_t + \varepsilon_{it}
equations.description: Fama-French three-factor model: asset excess return regressed on market, size (SMB), and value (HML) factors.

qa.question: What do the loadings s_i and h_i measure in FF3?
qa.answer: Sensitivity to the size factor (SMB) and value factor (HML) respectively

equations.label: eq:expected-return
equations.latex: E[R_i - R_f] = b_i \lambda_{MKT} + s_i \lambda_{SMB} + h_i \lambda_{HML} + r_i \lambda_{RMW} + c_i \lambda_{CMA}
equations.description: Expected excess return in FF5 cross-sectional model: factor loadings times factor risk premia $\lambda$.

equations.label: eq:alpha
equations.description: Jensen's alpha: the difference between a portfolio's expected excess return and the expected return predicted by the factor model.

equations.label: eq:grs
equations.latex: GRS = \frac{T}{N} \cdot \frac{T - N - K}{T - K - 1} \cdot \frac{\hat{\alpha}' \hat{\Sigma}^{-1} \hat{\alpha}}{1 + \bar{\mu}' \hat{\Omega}^{-1} \bar{\mu}} \sim F(N, T-N-K), \quad \hat{\Sigma} = \begin{bmatrix}\tilde{\sigma}_{11} & \cdots & \tilde{\sigma}_{1N} \\ \vdots & \ddots & \vdots \\ \tilde{\sigma}_{N1} & \cdots & \tilde{\sigma}_{NN}\end{bmatrix}
equations.description: Gibbons-Ross-Shanken (1989) test statistic. $\hat{\alpha}$ is the $N\times1$ vector of OLS intercepts, $\hat{\Sigma}$ is the residual covariance, $\bar{\mu}$ is the mean factor vector, and $K$ is the number of factors.

qa.question: What is the null hypothesis of the GRS test?
qa.answer: All N portfolio alphas are jointly equal to zero: alpha = 0

equations.label: eq:fama-macbeth
equations.description: Fama-MacBeth (1973) two-pass procedure: estimate betas in the first pass, then run monthly cross-sectional regressions to estimate risk premia $\hat{\lambda}_t$.

qa.question: How are standard errors computed in Fama-MacBeth?
qa.answer: Standard deviation of the time series of monthly cross-sectional estimates, divided by sqrt(T)

qa.question: What is the GRS statistic for FF5 on 25 size-BM portfolios?
Does FF5 explain momentum?
qa.answer: No, Winner and Loser portfolios have FF5 alphas of +0.74 and -0.74 percent per month respectively
